---
title: ""
knit: (function(input_file, encoding) {
  out_dir <- 'docs';
  rmarkdown::render(input_file,
 encoding=encoding,
 output_file=file.path(dirname(input_file), out_dir, 'geonet_template.html'))})
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
    css: "style.css"
---

<left>

# A GeoNet Visualization Template

Leo Furr and I develop a GeoNet Visualization Template to turn a geographic
network dataset into a coordinated family of maps and animations with minimal
configuration. Given a weighted, optionally directed edgelist over geographic
units &mdash; U.S. counties, commuting zones, or states &mdash; it produces
connectivity heatmaps, network-evolution animations, node-level heatmaps, and an
interactive web map, all from a single input. The template supports a
visualization-first workflow, in which a fast rendering of how the data unfold
across space serves as an initial diagnostic that surfaces data-quality gaps,
outliers, and candidate patterns before formal cleaning. Its parameterized
design also lets AI adapt it to virtually any geographic network dataset.

See the <a href="Furr_Tan_GeoNetViz_paper.pdf" target="_blank">extended abstract</a>
and the <a href="Furr_Tan_GeoNetViz_manual.pdf" target="_blank">manual</a>.

Below are example animations of U.S. county-to-county migration flows from the
Internal Revenue Service (IRS), produced with the GeoNet visualization template.

<br>

<style>
.geonet-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr);
  gap: 24px;
  max-width: 1100px;
}
.geonet-grid figure { margin: 0; }
.geonet-grid video {
  width: 100%;
  height: auto;
  border: 1px solid #ddd;
  border-radius: 6px;
}
.geonet-grid figcaption {
  margin-top: 8px;
  font-weight: bold;
  text-align: center;
}
@media (max-width: 700px) {
  .geonet-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
}
</style>

<div class="geonet-grid">

  <figure>
    <video controls loop muted playsinline preload="metadata">
      <source src="mig_raw_county_inflow.mp4" type="video/mp4">
      Your browser does not support the video tag.
    </video>
    <figcaption>County In-Migration</figcaption>
  </figure>

  <figure>
    <video controls loop muted playsinline preload="metadata">
      <source src="mig_raw_county_outflow.mp4" type="video/mp4">
      Your browser does not support the video tag.
    </video>
    <figcaption>County Out-Migration</figcaption>
  </figure>

  <figure>
    <video controls loop muted playsinline preload="metadata">
      <source src="mig_raw_county_net.mp4" type="video/mp4">
      Your browser does not support the video tag.
    </video>
    <figcaption>Net County Migration</figcaption>
  </figure>

  <figure>
    <video controls loop muted playsinline preload="metadata">
      <source src="mig_raw_county_backbone_network.mp4" type="video/mp4">
      Your browser does not support the video tag.
    </video>
    <figcaption>Migration Backbone Network</figcaption>
  </figure>

</div>

</left>
